ui_title: Residents aged 16+ looking after home or family
short_title: Looking after home or family
comparator_note: Compare selected neighbourhood(s) to borough and London using direct-labelled markers; preserve multi-borough logic.
trend_note: Trend not a default focus.
map_note: Map is optional and should always be paired with a ranked or benchmark comparison view.
axis_guidance: Use a shared baseline and consistent scale across comparators; marker charts may crop the axis if it improves readability without exaggeration.
labelling_guidance: Direct-label Selected, Borough and London markers; avoid legends.
accessibility_guidance: Keep backgrounds plain and gridlines light; do not rely on colour alone; prefer direct labels over legends; use accessible contrast and provide plain-language help text.
misinterpretation_risks: Snapshot comparisons show the latest available position; small differences may not be practically meaningful, so keep comparator labels and units explicit.
visual_rationale: Default to a direct comparison view because neighbourhood users usually need to know how the selected area compares with borough and London before exploring detail.
expert_review_questions: Does the scale exaggerate small differences or make very large benchmark gaps meaningless?
expert_review_amendment: Direct-label Selected, Borough and London and keep the scale consistent across similar indicators.
operational_review_notes: Lead with a simple sentence: above or below borough and London.
operational_review_amendment: Show simple comparator badges and a one-line interpretation first.
aggregation_warning: Recompute from category counts rather than averaging local shares.
methodology_summary: Aggregate residents looking after home or family and divide by the resident population aged 16 and over.
caveats: Static 2021 snapshot.
review_notes: Hidden from the public Explorer because this component is now covered by a richer profile indicator with composition toggles.
notes: Helps surface unpaid household and caring responsibilities that sit alongside paid work indicators.

ui_title: Area covered by flood areas
short_title: Area covered by flood areas
axis_guidance: Start bar charts at zero and keep any comparison axis consistent across areas.
labelling_guidance: Use direct labels and plain-English titles; avoid legends where the selection is small enough to label directly.
accessibility_guidance: Keep contrast strong, minimise clutter, and provide a ranked or benchmark alternative where a map is shown.
misinterpretation_risks: Users may over-read it as a prediction of actual flooding rather than contextual exposure.
visual_rationale: Overlap share can be mappable because it is normalised and spatially explicit.
aggregation_warning: Needs explicit spatial overlay logic and careful public explanation.
methodology_summary: Intersect flood-area polygons with neighbourhood boundaries and calculate a proportional overlap measure.
caveats: Represents flood-area context, not actual flooding or neighbourhood-level risk realised at a specific time.
notes: Implemented in the live app from the Environment Agency flood-area API as a contextual climate and flood-exposure overlay.

ui_title: Average annual daily motor traffic
short_title: Average annual daily motor traffic
source_name: Department for Transport Road Traffic Statistics API
axis_guidance: Start bar charts at zero and keep any comparison axis consistent across areas.
labelling_guidance: Use direct labels and plain-English titles; avoid legends where the selection is small enough to label directly.
accessibility_guidance: Keep contrast strong, minimise clutter, and provide a ranked or benchmark alternative where a map is shown.
misinterpretation_risks: Users may assume the value represents all traffic in the neighbourhood rather than sampled traffic-flow context.
visual_rationale: A direct comparison chart is easier to read than a map for a derived traffic-exposure measure.
aggregation_warning: Do not simply average point values; would need a clear summarisation rule and explainability guardrails.
methodology_summary: If used, summarise count-point traffic flow into a neighbourhood-context measure with explicit spatial rules.
caveats: Count points sample traffic locations rather than representing every street evenly.
notes: Implemented in the live app from the DfT road traffic API as an advanced sampled traffic-flow context measure.

ui_title: Bus stops
short_title: Bus stops
axis_guidance: Start bar charts at zero and keep any comparison axis consistent across areas.
labelling_guidance: Use direct labels and plain-English titles; avoid legends where the selection is small enough to label directly.
accessibility_guidance: Keep contrast strong, minimise clutter, and provide a ranked or benchmark alternative where a map is shown.
aggregation_warning: Better suited to advanced transport exploration than the default public landing view.

ui_title: Public transport stops and stations
short_title: Public transport stops and stations
axis_guidance: Start bar charts at zero and keep any comparison axis consistent across areas.
labelling_guidance: Use direct labels and plain-English titles; avoid legends where the selection is small enough to label directly.
accessibility_guidance: Keep contrast strong, minimise clutter, and provide a ranked or benchmark alternative where a map is shown.
misinterpretation_risks: Users may mistake raw node counts for access quality without considering population or area size.
visual_rationale: A benchmark comparison works well first, especially when paired later with a density alternative.
aggregation_warning: Raw counts can be shown, but density or per-population normalisation is often easier to interpret.
methodology_summary: Download access-node CSV, spatially join points to neighbourhoods, then sum counts.
caveats: Raw counts should be complemented by density or context notes because larger neighbourhoods can naturally contain more nodes.
notes: Implemented in the live app from the NaPTAN API as a direct neighbourhood transport-context measure.

ui_title: Public transport stops and stations density
short_title: Public transport stops and stations density
axis_guidance: Start bar charts at zero and keep any comparison axis consistent across areas.
labelling_guidance: Use direct labels and plain-English titles; avoid legends where the selection is small enough to label directly.
accessibility_guidance: Keep contrast strong, minimise clutter, and provide a ranked or benchmark alternative where a map is shown.
misinterpretation_risks: Users may read it as an all-purpose accessibility score rather than one transport-infrastructure proxy.
visual_rationale: Density is the more mappable and more comparable form of the access-node concept.

caveats: Density is more interpretable than raw count, but still not a full public transport accessibility score.
notes: Implemented in the live app from the NaPTAN API as a density-based neighbourhood transport-context measure.

ui_title: Cycle hire dock capacity
short_title: Cycle hire dock capacity
axis_guidance: Start bar charts at zero and keep any comparison axis consistent across areas.
labelling_guidance: Use direct labels and plain-English titles; avoid legends where the selection is small enough to label directly.
accessibility_guidance: Keep contrast strong, minimise clutter, and provide a ranked or benchmark alternative where a map is shown.
misinterpretation_risks: Users may assume a low value means poor overall transport access rather than lack of cycle-hire provision.
visual_rationale: A comparison view is clearer than a map for a small central-London infrastructure feature set.
aggregation_warning: Raw dock-capacity totals strongly favour central neighbourhoods with dense cycle-hire coverage.
methodology_summary: Download BikePoint locations, extract installed dock counts, spatially join to neighbourhoods, and sum total dock capacity.
caveats: Dock capacity reflects cycle-hire infrastructure only and should not be read as overall transport capacity.
notes: Implemented in the live app from the TfL BikePoint API as an advanced cycle-hire capacity measure.

comparator_note: Compare selected neighbourhood(s) to borough and London using direct-labelled markers; preserve multi-borough logic.
trend_note: Enable trend toggle when multiple periods are available; default to latest 12 to 24 periods for monthly series.
map_note: Map is optional and should always be paired with a ranked or benchmark comparison view.
axis_guidance: Time runs left to right; line charts may use a cropped y-axis if clearly labelled; keep 6 to 10 light gridlines on desktop.
labelling_guidance: Direct-label the selected series and key benchmarks where possible; avoid legends unless lines overlap.
accessibility_guidance: Keep backgrounds plain and gridlines light; do not rely on colour alone; prefer direct labels over legends; use accessible contrast and provide plain-language help text.
misinterpretation_risks: Short-term fluctuation can be noisy; use rolling average and annotate latest change carefully. Crime events are geocoded or anonymised and should be read as area context rather than exact incident locations.
visual_rationale: Use a grouped latest-period crime-mix chart so users can scan the balance of headline crime types without opening many separate indicator rows.
expert_review_questions: Is the y-axis helping the user see change without distorting magnitude?
expert_review_amendment: Use direct labels, light gridlines and a recent-change annotation rather than many markers.
operational_review_notes: Start on the latest period and show whether the line is rising, falling or flat.
operational_review_amendment: Default to latest 12 to 24 periods; hide older history behind expand.
aggregation_warning: Requires summing numerator and denominator before recomputing the rate.
methodology_summary: Use monthly neighbourhood crime rates and show the latest-period mix across headline crime categories as a grouped overview.
review_notes: Use this row as the public crime-type entry point; the grouped crime mix view uses the hidden sibling crime rows behind the scenes.
notes: Canonical public crime-type profile row. Hidden sibling crime-type indicators still support the grouped mix view.

ui_title: B13b - Reoffending levels: average number of reoffences per reoffender
short_title: B13b - Reoffending levels: average number of reoffences per reoffender
time_coverage: Annual. Data for this indicator are calculated by summing up the counts for the number of reoffenders and the number of reoffences that are recorded in the four quarterly cohorts that take place within the financial year. As an example, for the year 2016 to 2017, the four quarterly cohorts that are included are Apr 2016 to Jun 2016, Jul 2016 to Sep 2016, Oct 2016 to Dec 2016 and Jan 2017 to Mar 2017.Ministry of Justice advise to use annual data as a method of proven reoffending rather than using quarterly data, as quarterly data would not be possible to produce for some areas due to small cohort sizes. Annual data is also preferred to allow comparisons across years without the variability of the quarterly data.

short_title: B13a - Reoffending levels: percentage of offenders who reoffend
time_coverage: Annual.Data for this indicator are calculated by summing up the counts for the number of offenders and the number of reoffenders that are recorded in the four quarterly cohorts that take place within the financial year. As an example, for the year 2016 to 2017, the four quarterly cohorts that are included are Apr 2016 to Jun 2016, Jul 2016 to Sep 2016, Oct 2016 to Dec 2016 and Jan 2017 to Mar 2017.Ministry of Justice advise to use annual data as a method of proven reoffending rather than using quarterly data, as quarterly data would not be possible to produce for some areas due to small cohort sizes. Annual data is also preferred to allow comparisons across years without the variability of the quarterly data.

ui_title: Average domestic energy efficiency score
short_title: Average domestic energy efficiency score
time_coverage: Rolling certificate stock subject to refresh design
axis_guidance: Start bar charts at zero and keep any comparison axis consistent across areas.
labelling_guidance: Use direct labels and plain-English titles; avoid legends where the selection is small enough to label directly.
accessibility_guidance: Keep contrast strong, minimise clutter, and provide a ranked or benchmark alternative where a map is shown.
misinterpretation_risks: Certificates do not represent the full housing stock uniformly; older or missing certificates can bias area comparisons.
visual_rationale: A benchmark comparison explains the score clearly, while a map becomes useful only after the aggregation method is trusted.
aggregation_warning: Average only after defensible de-duplication, date handling, and certificate-to-neighbourhood assignment.
methodology_summary: Filter domestic EPC certificates, assign them to LSOAs or neighbourhoods, and average the standard energy-efficiency score.
caveats: Live EPC API requires credentials and certificate coverage is not the same as the full housing stock.
notes: Strong housing-quality candidate once credentialed access and certificate deduplication are in place.

axis_guidance: Start bar charts at zero and keep any comparison axis consistent across areas.

accessibility_guidance: Keep contrast strong, minimise clutter, and provide a ranked or benchmark alternative where a map is shown.

ui_title: Low-efficiency homes share
short_title: Low-efficiency homes share
axis_guidance: Start bar charts at zero and keep any comparison axis consistent across areas.
labelling_guidance: Use direct labels and plain-English titles; avoid legends where the selection is small enough to label directly.
accessibility_guidance: Keep contrast strong, minimise clutter, and provide a ranked or benchmark alternative where a map is shown.

methodology_summary: Count lower-band certificates and divide by the full domestic certificate stock in each area.
caveats: Certificate coverage varies and may not track all homes evenly.
notes: Clearer for public users than average score alone because it speaks directly to poorer-quality housing stock.

misinterpretation_risks: Counts are driven by population size; pair with a rate or percentage where possible and avoid choropleth maps for raw counts. Short-term fluctuation can be noisy; use rolling average and annotate latest change carefully. Crime events are geocoded or anonymised and should be read as area context rather than exact incident locations.
visual_rationale: Default to trend because the time pattern is the main story and the latest-point comparison can sit alongside it.

accessibility_guidance: Keep contrast strong, minimise clutter, and provide a ranked or benchmark alternative where a map is shown.

caveats: Neighbourhood density is derived from LSOA density and area, so it should be read as a broad settlement-pattern measure rather than crowding inside homes.
notes: Uses the supplied Census density table and a proper area-weighted roll-up so the neighbourhood value remains methodologically sound.

ui_title: Criminal damage and arson per 1,000 residents
short_title: Criminal damage per 1,000
comparator_note: Compare selected neighbourhood(s) to borough and London using direct-labelled markers; preserve multi-borough logic.
trend_note: Enable trend toggle when multiple periods are available; default to latest 12 to 24 periods for monthly series.
map_note: Map is optional and should always be paired with a ranked or benchmark comparison view.
axis_guidance: Time runs left to right; line charts may use a cropped y-axis if clearly labelled; keep 6 to 10 light gridlines on desktop.
labelling_guidance: Direct-label the selected series and key benchmarks where possible; avoid legends unless lines overlap.
accessibility_guidance: Keep backgrounds plain and gridlines light; do not rely on colour alone; prefer direct labels over legends; use accessible contrast and provide plain-language help text.
misinterpretation_risks: Short-term fluctuation can be noisy; use rolling average and annotate latest change carefully. Crime events are geocoded or anonymised and should be read as area context rather than exact incident locations.
visual_rationale: Default to trend because the time pattern is the main story and the latest-point comparison can sit alongside it.
expert_review_questions: Is the y-axis helping the user see change without distorting magnitude?
expert_review_amendment: Use direct labels, light gridlines and a recent-change annotation rather than many markers.
operational_review_notes: Start on the latest period and show whether the line is rising, falling or flat.
operational_review_amendment: Default to latest 12 to 24 periods; hide older history behind expand.
aggregation_warning: Requires summing numerator and denominator before recomputing the rate.
methodology_summary: Built by summing numerators and denominators across contributing Crime event with LSOA codes and recomputing the rate.
review_notes: New indicator added to complete the crime type bundle.
notes: Current live indicator in the explorer, built from a static/local file ingest. Closest repeatable API route: data.police.uk API.

map_note: Map is optional and should always be paired with a ranked or benchmark comparison view.
axis_guidance: Time runs left to right; line charts may use a cropped y-axis if clearly labelled; keep 6 to 10 light gridlines on desktop.
labelling_guidance: Direct-label the selected series and key benchmarks where possible; avoid legends unless lines overlap.
accessibility_guidance: Keep backgrounds plain and gridlines light; do not rely on colour alone; prefer direct labels over legends; use accessible contrast and provide plain-language help text.
misinterpretation_risks: Short-term fluctuation can be noisy; use rolling average and annotate latest change carefully. Crime events are geocoded or anonymised and should be read as area context rather than exact incident locations.
visual_rationale: Default to trend because the time pattern is the main story and the latest-point comparison can sit alongside it.
expert_review_questions: Is the y-axis helping the user see change without distorting magnitude?
expert_review_amendment: Use direct labels, light gridlines and a recent-change annotation rather than many markers.
operational_review_notes: Start on the latest period and show whether the line is rising, falling or flat.
operational_review_amendment: Default to latest 12 to 24 periods; hide older history behind expand.
aggregation_warning: Requires summing numerator and denominator before recomputing the rate.
methodology_summary: Built by summing numerators and denominators across contributing Crime event with LSOA codes and recomputing the rate.
review_notes: New indicator added to complete the crime type bundle.

ui_title: Atrial fibrillation prevalence (QOF)
short_title: Prevalence
source_name: NHS England Quality and Outcomes Framework
comparator_note: Uses the neighbourhood selection, borough comparator where valid, and London benchmark.
trend_note: Annual QOF trend view across the supplied years.
map_note: Map is provided as a secondary spatial view for this estimated rate.
axis_guidance: Use percentages on a zero-based axis for ranked or benchmark comparisons.
labelling_guidance: Prefer direct labels for the selected neighbourhoods and benchmarks rather than legends.
accessibility_guidance: Keep comparator counts low and avoid relying on hover alone for key values.
misinterpretation_risks: This is a neighbourhood estimate derived from GP-practice data rather than a directly observed resident value.
visual_rationale: Use a direct benchmark comparison first for the latest year, with a trend view for change over time and a map for local spatial pattern checking.
aggregation_warning: Do not average local shares directly; recompute from summed numerators and denominators.
methodology_summary: Allocate GP-practice QOF prevalence to LSOAs using practice-to-LSOA registrations plus LSOA age structure, then recompute neighbourhood rates from summed numerators and denominators.
caveats: Indicative neighbourhood estimate rather than a directly observed resident measure.
review_notes: Generated from the QOF build pipeline.
notes: Built from local QOF raw files plus NHS GP registration support files and Census-based age allocation.
